af_score_components.notes: No MCP server (use kubectl-mcp-server for MCP access). Kubernetes REST API — declarative resource management, pod operations, scaling, RBAC. Service account (recommended for agents) or bearer token + RBAC. Rate limiting from API server (--max-requests-inflight). kubectl apply is idempotent — safe for agents. Field managers for server-side apply. Watch API for event-driven agents. Extensive resource types require domain knowledge. Use admission webhooks carefully — they affect all resource creates/updates.

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS required for all API server connections. Service account tokens + RBAC with per-resource, per-verb, per-namespace granularity — gold standard for least-privilege. CNCF project with vendor backing (Google, Red Hat, VMware). SOC2/ISO27001 on managed platforms (GKE, EKS, AKS). Service account tokens via projected volumes (auto-rotated). PodSecurity admission controller. OIDC for external identity providers. Excellent security architecture.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted or managed (EKS, GKE, AKS) — managed platforms have SLA. Stable versioned API with 12-18 month deprecation periods. Good API migration guides. Retry with exponential backoff recommended. Server-side apply is idempotent. kube-apiserver rate limits (429) require backoff. Watch events may miss events without resourceVersion tracking. etcd is the reliability bottleneck for self-hosted clusters.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Lacework cloud security and compliance platform. Simple API key. Alerts, assessments, compliance reports, agent data, container security — all accessible via API. Rate limits documented but vary by endpoint. Complex data models for cloud security events. Webhook support for alert notifications. Good for security automation agents — automated alert triage, compliance reporting. Enterprise pricing limits accessibility for evaluation.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. API key auth — limited scope granularity. Lacework maintained. SOC2/ISO27001/GDPR compliance for security platform. API key via environment variable. Security event data is highly sensitive — access control and audit logging critical. Agent managing security events must itself be secure. No read-only API key option documented — full account access per key.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: SaaS with SLA. Stable API versioning. Good migration guides. Retry guidance documented. Rate limits per endpoint vary — test before building production workflows. Security event data volume can be large — pagination required. Webhook delivery reliable for alert notifications. Enterprise deployment means onboarding time before API access is available.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Lambda Labs GPU cloud API — on-demand and reserved GPU instances. Simple API key. Instance launch, status, terminate. SSH key management via API. File system management for persistent storage. Rate limits documented. GPU availability is highly variable — no guarantees on specific GPU types. Instance launch is async — must poll status. Good for ML training automation agents. Cost management critical — GPU instances are expensive.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Simple API key — no scope granularity. Lambda Labs maintained. No formal compliance certifications listed. API key via environment variable. GPU instance launch has high financial blast radius — agents can launch expensive instances. No read-only mode available. SSH keys managed via API — must protect private keys. Billing alerts recommended for agent-managed GPU workloads.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: SaaS with status page. Stable API versioning. Good migration guides. Retry guidance documented. GPU availability not guaranteed — specific types (H100, A100) may be unavailable. Instance launch async — poll until running. Reserved instances provide more reliable GPU access for production. Spot-like pricing means instances can be terminated with notice. File systems persist between instance runs — manage lifecycle carefully.

af_score_components.notes: MCP server with very limited quality (45). No authentication required. Extremely low existing security score (20) indicates severe security concerns. Very limited documentation. Error handling absent or minimal. Rate limits unknown. Cannot recommend for any production use without complete security audit. Purpose unclear from available information. High operational risk.

security_score_components.security_notes: CRITICAL: No authentication required, no TLS enforcement documented, no scope control. Unknown dependency hygiene. This tool should not be used in any production environment. Security score of 20 in existing evaluation reflects fundamental security deficiencies. Full security audit required before any use. Not recommended for agent workflows.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted with no SLA. Unknown version. No documented change policy. Unknown idempotency behavior. Minimal error recovery. Not suitable for any production agent workflow. Early-stage or experimental tool with significant risks.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. LangSmith LLM observability and evaluation platform by LangChain. Simple API key. Trace logging, evaluation runs, dataset management, prompt management. Good for monitoring LLM agent performance. Tracing adds minimal latency overhead. Dataset creation for regression testing. Evaluation runs for quality assessment. Rate limits by plan. Good documentation with LangChain integration guides. Useful for agents that need to track and improve their own performance.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Simple API key — no scope granularity. LangChain maintained. No formal compliance certifications listed. API key via environment variable. All LLM traces (prompts and responses) sent to LangSmith — review data policy for sensitive agent conversations. Self-hosted LangSmith available for data privacy requirements. Traces contain potentially sensitive application logic.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: SaaS with status page. Stable API versioning. Good migration guides. Retry guidance documented. Trace upload failures should not block agent execution — use async/fire-and-forget tracing. Rate limits by plan constrain high-volume agent tracing. Dataset and evaluation operations idempotent. LangChain SDK version compatibility affects tracing behavior.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Lark (Bytedance) team collaboration platform — messages, documents, meetings, tasks. OAuth2 or App ID + App Secret. Rate limits documented per API. Message sending, document management, calendar integration. OpenAPI schema available. Similar to Slack/Teams but with more integrated document capabilities. Primarily used in Asian markets — documentation partially translated to English. Webhook support for event-driven agents.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. OAuth2 with Lark-specific permission scopes or App credentials. Moderate scope granularity. Bytedance maintained. GDPR compliance for EU data. App credentials via environment variables. Bytedance data residency considerations — review for regulated industries. Webhook signature verification required for production. Token management for different auth flows adds complexity.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: SaaS with SLA. Stable API versioning. Good migration guides. Retry guidance documented. Rate limits per endpoint — test before production. Webhook delivery reliable with retry. Message sending not idempotent — retrying sends duplicates. OAuth token refresh required for long-running agents. Documentation quality varies — English translations may lag Chinese originals.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. LaunchDarkly feature flag management API. API key or OAuth2. Feature flag CRUD, targeting rules, segments, environments. Rate limits documented (100 req/min on personal tokens, higher for service tokens). Service tokens for agents — preferred over personal tokens. Flag evaluation via SDK (not REST API) for production code. Webhooks for flag change events. Patch operations for atomic flag updates. Excellent for feature flag automation agents.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Service tokens (API key) with per-environment permissions or OAuth2 with project scopes. Per-environment token restriction available — excellent for least-privilege staging vs production separation. LaunchDarkly maintained. SOC2/ISO27001/GDPR compliance. API key via environment variable. Feature flag changes can affect production traffic — use staging environment tokens for testing. Environment-level tokens prevent cross-environment blast radius.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: SaaS with SLA and status page. Stable versioned API. Excellent migration guides. Retry guidance documented. Patch operations atomic — flag updates safe to retry with idempotent changes. Environment-level versioning for flag changes. Webhook delivery reliable. Flag evaluation via SDK is separate from management API — SDK has streaming for low-latency flag updates. Rate limits by token type require planning.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server, no authentication required. Existing security score (0) indicates this tool has no documented security controls. Very limited publicly available documentation. Purpose and capabilities unclear from available metadata. Cannot recommend for agent integration without complete evaluation. Likely an internal tool, early-stage prototype, or improperly configured service. Exercise extreme caution.

security_score_components.security_notes: CRITICAL: No authentication and no documented security controls. Security score of 0 indicates fundamental security deficiencies. No scope control or access management. Unknown dependency hygiene. Not suitable for any production use. Complete security review required before considering deployment. Not recommended for agent workflows.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: No SLA or reliability documentation available. Unknown version. No documented change policy. Unknown idempotency behavior. Cannot assess reliability without more information. Not suitable for production agent workflows.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Lemon Squeezy (now part of Stripe ecosystem) digital commerce platform. Simple API key. Product, order, subscription, customer management. Rate limits documented. Webhook support for purchase events. License key management for software products. VAT handling built in (good for EU digital goods). Webhook signature verification available. Good for indie developer product automation agents.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Simple API key — no scope granularity. Lemon Squeezy/Stripe maintained. No formal compliance certifications listed beyond PCI compliance for payments. API key via environment variable. Payment and customer data handling requires PCI compliance. Webhook signature verification available — use in production. No read-only API key option.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: SaaS with SLA. Stable API versioning. Good migration guides. Retry guidance documented. Order creation not idempotent — retrying can create duplicate orders. Subscription management idempotent for status updates. Webhook delivery reliable with retry. Rate limits documented — design agent workflows accordingly. Integration with Stripe ecosystem improves reliability posture.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Let's Encrypt ACME protocol API for automated SSL/TLS certificate issuance. JWK (JSON Web Key) authentication via account keypair. Rate limits documented (50 certs/domain/week, 5 failures/hr). DNS-01 and HTTP-01 challenge types for domain validation. Certificate issuance is automated but requires challenge completion. ACME clients (Certbot, acme.sh, etc.) abstract the protocol complexity. Staging environment available. Excellent for certificate automation agents.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS required — this IS the TLS certificate issuance service. JWK asymmetric key authentication — stronger than symmetric API keys. Per-domain authorization via ACME challenges. ISRG (nonprofit) maintained. No compliance certifications (it IS the certificate authority). Account private key must be carefully managed — loss means creating new ACME account. Challenge solving requires DNS or HTTP control — proves domain ownership. CAA records add additional security.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Globally distributed infrastructure with excellent track record. ACME protocol is an internet standard (RFC 8555). Good migration documentation. Retry with exponential backoff documented. Challenge validation is idempotent on retry. Rate limits (50/week/domain, 5 failures/hr) require planning for bulk certificate management. Staging environment for testing prevents wasting production rate limits. Certificate renewals should happen 30+ days before expiry for buffer.
